title: A Treatise on the Astrolabe
summary: The repository XML is a local copy or version of U-M CME item ChaucerAstr, keyed by OCR from Walter W. Skeat's 1872 EETS edition of Chaucer's Treatise on the Astrolabe. Skeat's text uses Cambridge University Library MS Dd.3.53 for the Prologue, Part I.1–23, and Part II.1–40, then appends conclusions from St John's College Cambridge MS E.2, Bodleian MS Digby 72, and Bodleian MS Bodley 619. Modern scholarship generally treats the conclusions after II.40 as supplementary and probably non-Chaucerian, while leaving the precise authentic boundary open.
review_notes: The four manuscript witnesses have explicitly different section scopes; no one manuscript is the source of the whole electronic text.
Authorship of supplementary conclusions II.41–51 is separated from the secure attribution of the Prologue, Part I, and Part II.1–40.
The local 1997 availability notice and U-M's current No Copyright–United States statement are recorded independently.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Dd.3.53
entities.description: Skeat's base witness for the Prologue, Part I.1–23, and Part II.1–40; Skeat describes it as a vellum manuscript with diagrams.

holding.notes: Exact online catalog or digitization record was not verified.

notes: Skeat's introduction reports diagrams in the witness.

entities.label: St John's College Cambridge MS E.2
entities.description: Source in Skeat's edition for conclusions II.41–43 and II.46–50.

holding.notes: Exact online catalog or digitization record was not verified.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Digby 72
entities.description: Source in Skeat's edition for conclusions II.44–45.

holding.notes: The online Digby collection record confirms the shelfmark range, but an item-level catalogue or image record was not verified.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Bodley 619
entities.description: Source in Skeat's edition for conclusion II.51.

holding.notes: An item-level public catalogue or image record was not verified.

scope.notes: The XML says front matter and textual notes were excluded while source organization and square brackets were retained.

access.access_method: Public scan with exact direct PDF download

local_copies.label: Skeat Astrolabe 1872 complete scan
notes: Complete 220-page provider PDF, retaining scan matter, preface, edited Middle English text, critical and additional notes, Latin comparison text, glossary, publication lists, and library matter.

work_portion.label: Skeat's edited Middle English Treatise on the Astrolabe
locators: Prologue; Part I; Part II.1-46 with supplied sections
notes: Direct page inspection places the opening Prologue at PDF page 89 and the end of the edited Middle English text at PDF page 148; critical notes begin on PDF page 149.

access.format: Google Books/Internet Archive complete page-image PDF
notes: The ignored local cache is a complete scan of the printed edition, not a manuscript facsimile and not a separate grant of scan-image reuse rights.

notes: No complete public facsimile was verified in this research pass.

notes: No complete public facsimile or item-specific image terms were verified in this research pass.

notes: No complete public facsimile was verified in this research pass.

rights.component: Medieval text attributed to Chaucer and anonymous supplementary conclusions
rights.basis: The underlying texts were composed and copied centuries before modern copyright.
First published in 1872; Skeat died in 1912.

rights.component: Materials presented in U-M CME item ChaucerAstr

notes: The XML's 1997 availability notice prohibits redistribution by commercial and not-for-profit content providers; whether the current statement supersedes it is unresolved.

rights.basis: The underlying text and edition are public domain in the United States, but the XML embeds an express redistribution restriction.

editorial_practices.description: Skeat constructs the printed text from Dd.3.53 for the core and three other manuscripts for named supplementary conclusions.
editorial_practices.scope: Prologue, Parts I–II, with the precise witness scope recorded in relations

editorial_practices.description: The electronic edition excludes the printed front matter and textual notes, retains source organization, and retains square brackets.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/ChaucerAstr.xml, HEADER, editorial declaration, and ANA witness labels.
evidence.summary: Identifies the 1997 OCR encoding, Skeat 1872 source, omissions, retained brackets, source organization, and section-by-section manuscript assignments.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, CME item ChaucerAstr.
evidence.summary: Confirms Chaucer attribution, Skeat's 1872 EETS edition, public full text, structure, and current NoC-US statement.

evidence.citation: Walter W. Skeat, ed., A Treatise on the Astrolabe, EETS Extra Series 16 (1872), public scan.
evidence.summary: Public reproduction of the immediate source edition, including its manuscript-derived text and editorial apparatus.

evidence.citation: Walter W. Skeat, introduction to A Treatise on the Astrolabe, reproduced in Chaucer's Works, vol. 3.
evidence.summary: Describes Dd.3.53 as Skeat's base manuscript and notes its vellum support and diagrams; this is a later reproduction of Skeat rather than an independent modern catalog.

evidence.citation: Catherine Eagleton, ‘Chaucer's astrolabe treatise: a reassessment,’ Journal for the History of Astronomy 38 (2007).
evidence.summary: Supports Chaucer's authorship of the core treatise and its translation and reworking of standard Latin astrolabe materials, including pseudo-Messahalla and Sacrobosco traditions.

evidence.citation: The Medieval Review, review of A Treatise on the Astrolabe, Variorum Edition of the Works of Geoffrey Chaucer.
evidence.summary: Reports the scholarly problem that material after Part II.40 is generally supplementary and probably not Chaucer's, while the exact stopping point remains unresolved.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Manuscripts and University Archives.
evidence.summary: Provides the repository contact and access route for manuscript enquiries; no exact Dd.3.53 public facsimile was verified here.

evidence.citation: St John's College, Cambridge, Old Library and contact information.
evidence.summary: Provides the current appointment and enquiry route for researchers consulting St John's Special Collections; it does not supply an E.2 item record or image reuse license.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Digby Manuscripts collection record.
evidence.summary: Confirms the institutional Digby manuscript collection and its shelfmark range; it is a collection-level record, not an item-level description of Digby 72 or Bodley 619.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Using and ordering special collections.
evidence.summary: Provides current reader, enquiry, and manuscript-request routes for Bodleian Special Collections.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Reproducing images from Imaging Services.
evidence.summary: Explains that supplied images and reproductions carry Bodleian terms, distinguishes non-commercial academic use from other reuse, and provides copying and permission routes.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.summary: Supports the U.S. public-domain term assessment for an edition first published in 1872.

open_questions.question: Which, if any, conclusions after Part II.40 are Chaucer's rather than later supplements?
next_steps: Consult the Variorum textual introduction and current manuscript scholarship; preserve the core/supplement distinction in display and attribution.

open_questions.question: Which individual readings, brackets, diagrams, and omissions in the XML reflect Skeat's intervention, manuscript evidence, or OCR correction?
next_steps: Collate the XML against the 1872 page images and then against the four manuscripts or licensed reproductions.

open_questions.question: Are complete public digital facsimiles now available for Dd.3.53, E.2, Digby 72, or Bodley 619?
next_steps: Search each repository catalog by exact shelfmark and contact the holding library about digitization or reproduction.

open_questions.question: Does U-M's current NoC-US statement supersede the 1997 XML restriction against redistribution by commercial or not-for-profit content providers?
next_steps: Obtain a written interpretation from U-M before redistributing the XML or a close derivative commercially.
Keep copyright status and provider-contract status separate.
